events.title: Time Series As A Unit Square: Towards a Compute Efficient Foundation Model (Devavrat Shah, MIT)
events.description: This academic lecture explores advanced concepts in developing compute-efficient foundation models using time series data. The presentation features insights from Devavrat Shah of MIT.

events.title: Biohacking: Law, Food Systems, and the Future of Optimization
events.description: A discussion on the intersection of biohacking, legal frameworks, food systems, and future optimization strategies. This event delves into the ethical and practical implications of biological advancements.

events.title: Louise Mozingo | Public Land, Public Space, Public Discourse
events.description: Join Louise Mozingo for a lecture on the critical role of public land and space in shaping public discourse and community engagement. The event explores how these areas influence societal interactions.

events.title: Neural Data Science: Center for Neural Data Science Seminar with Tim Currier
events.description: A seminar by Tim Currier from the Center for Neural Data Science, discussing recent advancements and research in neural data science. This event is part of the ongoing seminar series.

events.title: Bate-Papo
events.description: A casual 'Bate-Papo' (chat) session, providing an opportunity for informal discussion and networking. This event fosters community and conversation among attendees.

events.title: The Inaugural UC Berkeley School of Public Health John Swartzberg Lecture
events.organizer: UC Berkeley School of Public Health
events.description: The inaugural lecture of the Dr. John Swartzberg Annual Lecture series, featuring Dr. Erica Pan, Director and State Public Health Officer for the California Department of Public Health.

events.title: Kamran Anwar | Songs of the Sufi: The Untold Story of Classical Qawwali
events.description: Kamran Anwar presents 'Songs of the Sufi,' exploring the untold history and cultural significance of classical Qawwali music. This lecture offers a deep dive into its origins and evolution.

events.title: 2026 Herb Caen Lecture: Press Freedom in Peril
events.description: The annual Herb Caen Lecture focuses on 'Press Freedom in Peril,' examining contemporary challenges and threats to journalistic independence. This significant event addresses critical issues facing media today.

events.title: Gilead Lecture in Organic Chemistry: Ana Bahamonde
events.description: Ana Bahamonde, Assistant Professor of Chemistry at UC Riverside, delivers the Gilead Lecture in Organic Chemistry. The seminar covers cutting-edge research in organic chemistry.

events.title: Harold S. Johnston Lecture
events.description: The annual Harold S. Johnston Lecture hosted by the College of Chemistry, featuring a prominent speaker in the field. This lecture honors significant contributions to chemical sciences.

events.title: Fostering Multilingual Learning for Child Thriving
events.organizer: UC Berkeley School of Public Health
events.description: A conversation exploring multilingual development, language, identity, and the policy conditions needed to sustain linguistically affirming early learning environments.

events.title: Zoonotic and Environmental Dimensions of Human Urinary Tract Infections
events.organizer: UC Berkeley School of Public Health
events.description: An online presentation on the zoonotic and environmental factors contributing to human urinary tract infections. This seminar highlights critical public health research.

events.title: 2026 David E. Nelson Memorial Lecture
events.location: Goldberg Room 297, UC Berkeley School of Law
events.description: The David E. Nelson Memorial Lecture examines the intersection of business, law, and market dominance in the digital economy. Speakers explore the clash between innovation and regulation.

events.title: Moving Inland: Coastal and Mountain Landscapes in Cyprus' Hunter-Gatherer Past
events.description: A lecture discussing the archaeological findings and environmental history of Cyprus' hunter-gatherer societies, focusing on their adaptation to diverse landscapes.

events.title: Arbitraging China's Platform Economy in the Silicon Savannah
events.description: This presentation explores the dynamics of China's platform economy and its influence on emerging markets, particularly in the 'Silicon Savannah'.

events.title: Ableism When Accessing the Past: Studies of Disability in the Ancient World (Hannah Vogel)
events.description: Hannah Vogel delivers a lecture on studies of disability in the ancient world, examining historical perspectives on ableism and accessibility.

events.title: Layli Long Soldier: Poetry Reading
events.description: A poetry reading by acclaimed poet Layli Long Soldier, sharing her works and insights. This event highlights contemporary indigenous poetry.

events.title: Bioengineering Seminar: Engineering Human Blood Vessels for Modeling and Therapeutics
events.description: A seminar focusing on the engineering of human blood vessels for advanced medical modeling and therapeutic applications. This event showcases interdisciplinary research.

events.title: Tibor Szilvasi: Enabling the Next Level of Understanding in Heterogeneous Catalysis Using Machine Learning Interatomic Potentials
events.description: Tibor Szilvasi presents research on leveraging machine learning interatomic potentials to advance the understanding of heterogeneous catalysis. This seminar highlights cutting-edge computational chemistry.

events.title: Language and Colonial Legacies in Portuguese Higher Education: The Experiences of Brazilian and African Portuguese-Speaking Students
events.description: A discussion on the impact of colonial legacies on language and education within Portuguese higher education, focusing on the experiences of Brazilian and African Portuguese-speaking students.

events.title: Robert Habeck and Andrea Paluch | Writing Together - How We Did It
events.description: Robert Habeck and Andrea Paluch share their collaborative writing process and experiences in a joint presentation. This event offers insights into their creative partnership.

events.title: 2026 Fotovision Lecture: An Evening with Ed Kashi
events.description: An evening with renowned photojournalist Ed Kashi as part of the 2026 Fotovision Lecture series. Kashi shares his work and experiences in visual storytelling.

events.title: Creative Wellness: Supporting Holistic Mental Health for Underrepresented Minority Graduate Students
events.description: A workshop focused on creative wellness strategies to support the holistic mental health of underrepresented minority graduate students. The session aims to provide valuable resources and community.

events.title: Berkeley Art, Law, and Finance Project: Legal Provenance and TWAIL: A Discussion
events.description: A discussion hosted by the Berkeley Art, Law, and Finance Project on legal provenance and Third World Approaches to International Law (TWAIL). This online event delves into complex legal and cultural issues.

events.title: Incommunicable: Toward Communicative Justice in Health and Medicine (Authors Meet Critics panel)
events.description: An Authors Meet Critics panel discussing Charles Briggs' book 'Incommunicable: Toward Communicative Justice in Health and Medicine.' The event features insights from distinguished scholars.

events.title: BCLB Business Law Practice Area Workshop: The Digital Deal – AI's Impact on Real Estate Transactions with Gibson Dunn
events.description: This workshop explores the evolving landscape of real estate transactions in the age of artificial intelligence, with insights from Gibson Dunn. It examines AI's impact on legal practices.

events.title: EECS Colloquium | Can AI assist in Mathematics and Computer Science research?
events.description: A colloquium exploring the potential of AI to assist and advance research in mathematics and computer science. The event features discussions on AI's applications and future.

events.title: TIE Seminar Presentation (Geoffrey Borchhardt)
events.organizer: UC Berkeley Haas, Entrepreneurship & Innovation Group
events.description: Geoffrey Borchhardt presents research as part of the Entrepreneurship and Innovation Seminar series. This seminar offers insights into current topics in entrepreneurship.

events.title: Human-AI Interaction & Trust in Safety-Critical Domains
events.organizer: UC Berkeley School of Public Health
events.description: A seminar focusing on the critical aspects of human-AI interaction and building trust in safety-critical applications. The event brings together experts to discuss the challenges and solutions.

events.title: 29th Annual BTLJ-BCLT Spring Symposium: Origins, Evolution, and Possible Futures of the 1976 Copyright Act
events.location: Chevron Auditorium, International House, UC Berkeley
events.description: This symposium marks the 50th anniversary of the Copyright Act of 1976, examining its origins, reforms, and future challenges due to technological changes. The event fosters discussion among legal scholars.

events.title: Inorganic Chemistry Lecture: Patrick Holland
events.description: Patrick Holland, Conkey P. Whitehead Professor of Chemistry at Yale University, delivers an Inorganic Chemistry Lecture. The presentation focuses on recent research and advancements in the field.

events.title: Energy Markets Workshop – Omer Karaduman, Stanford University
events.organizer: Energy Institute at Haas, UC Berkeley
events.description: Omer Karaduman from Stanford University leads an Energy Markets Workshop, discussing current trends and research in energy economics. This workshop provides insights into market dynamics.

events.title: CRELS speaker series with Simone Zhang: "Scoring Justice: Risk Assessment Tools, Court Practices, and Fairness Perceptions"
events.description: Simone Zhang presents on 'Scoring Justice,' discussing risk assessment tools, court practices, and fairness perceptions in this CRELS speaker series event.

events.title: Book presentation for P FKN R: How Bad Bunny Became the Global Voice of Puerto Rican Resistance
events.description: A book presentation for 'P FKN R: How Bad Bunny Became the Global Voice of Puerto Rican Resistance,' exploring the artist's cultural impact and political message.

events.title: Music Studies Colloquium: Birgit Lodes
events.description: Birgit Lodes presents in the Music Studies Colloquium, offering insights into her research in musicology. This event is part of the department's ongoing scholarly series.

events.title: TIE Seminar Presentation (Samuel Goldberg)
events.organizer: UC Berkeley Haas, Entrepreneurship & Innovation Group
events.description: Samuel Goldberg delivers a presentation as part of the Entrepreneurship and Innovation Seminar. The seminar explores new research and ideas in entrepreneurship and technology.

events.title: TIE Seminar Presentation (Lee Fleming + Saqib Chou...)
events.organizer: UC Berkeley Haas, Entrepreneurship & Innovation Group
events.description: Lee Fleming and Saqib Chou present their work in the Entrepreneurship and Innovation Seminar, offering diverse perspectives on technological innovation and market dynamics.

events.title: 5th Annual Normative Foundations of the Market Workshop
events.organizer: Berkeley Center for Private Law Theory (UC Berkeley Law)
events.description: The 5th Annual Normative Foundations of the Market Workshop brings together scholars to discuss the ethical and theoretical underpinnings of market structures.

events.title: Energy Markets Workshop – Aaron Smith, UC Berkeley
events.organizer: Energy Institute at Haas, UC Berkeley
events.description: Aaron Smith of UC Berkeley leads an Energy Markets Workshop, exploring current research and analysis of energy sector economics. This workshop contributes to ongoing energy discussions.

events.title: Public Contracts Colloquium
events.description: A colloquium dedicated to public contracts, bringing together legal scholars and practitioners to discuss significant issues and developments in the field.

events.title: David Finckel, cello and Wu Han, piano
events.description: An intimate concert featuring world-renowned cellist David Finckel and pianist Wu Han. Their performance promises an evening of exceptional classical music.

events.title: Conversación | Chile hoy: una conversación con Javiera Manzi Araneda y Claudio Alvarado Lincopi
events.description: A conversation titled 'Chile Hoy' featuring Javiera Manzi Araneda and Claudio Alvarado Lincopi, offering perspectives on contemporary Chilean culture and society.

events.title: Kaffeeklatsch
events.description: A Kaffeeklatsch event, offering a relaxed gathering for coffee and conversation. This social occasion fosters community and informal interaction.

events.title: Carla Shapreau | The Nazi-Era Theft of the 1709 Mendelssohn Stradivari – A Case Study on Looted Cultural Property, Provenance, and the Law
events.description: Carla Shapreau discusses her discovery of the 1709 Mendelssohn Stradivari violin, looted during WWII. The case study delves into stolen cultural property, provenance, and international law.

events.title: Noon Concert: UC Berkeley Wind Ensemble
events.description: The UC Berkeley Wind Ensemble performs a diverse program including works by Gustav Holst, Zhou Tian, and Mark Camphouse. Matthew Sadowski conducts this captivating noon concert.

events.title: Lunch Poems: Tonya Foster
events.description: Berkeley's renowned Lunch Poems series presents Tonya Foster for a reading of her poetry. The event offers a lunchtime cultural experience in a historic campus setting.

events.title: Composition Colloquium: Tacet(i) Ensemble
events.description: Tacet(i) Ensemble, Southeast Asia's leading contemporary music ensemble, presents a composition colloquium. The event highlights innovative approaches to music creation and performance.

events.title: UC Berkeley Chamber Chorus, University Chorus, UCBSO - Verdi, Requiem
events.description: The UC Berkeley Chamber Chorus, University Chorus, and UC Berkeley Symphony Orchestra perform Verdi's Requiem. Wei Cheng conducts this grand choral and orchestral work.

events.title: Forest Stearns Art Exhibition: The Intention to DRAWEVERYWHERE (Opening)
events.location: Julia Morgan Hall, UC Botanical Garden at Berkeley
events.description: Opening of 'The Intention to DRAWEVERYWHERE,' a multi-dimensional art exhibition by Bay Area artist Forest Stearns. The exhibition is hosted in the beautiful setting of the Botanical Garden.

events.title: African Music Ensemble: Spirit of Africa
events.description: The African Music Ensemble presents 'Spirit of Africa,' directed by C.K. Ladzekpo. This vibrant performance showcases traditional and contemporary African musical forms.

events.title: Plein AiR Patrol Blitz with Forest Stearns
events.description: All are invited to draw and paint en plein air at the UC Botanical Garden with artist Forest Stearns, celebrating his exhibition 'The Intention to DRAWEVERYWHERE.'

events.title: Butterfly Walk in the Garden
events.description: A guided butterfly walk through the UC Botanical Garden, offering an opportunity to observe local butterfly species in their natural habitat.

events.title: Spring Workshop Performance: “Whatever You Want, Man” By Erin Weitzman
events.organizer: Department of Theater, Dance, and Performance Studies
events.description: Erin Weitzman's 'Whatever You Want, Man' is an adaptation of Shakespeare's As You Like It, blending classic text with modern dialogue. This workshop performance explores comedic and imaginative themes.

events.title: Latin American and Caribbean Music Ensemble
events.description: The Latin American and Caribbean Music Ensemble performs, tapping into the rich genealogy of musics from across the Americas and the Caribbean.

events.title: Forest Stearns Exhibition – Meet the Artist Open House
events.description: An open house event to meet artist Forest Stearns as part of his exhibition 'The Intention to DRAWEVERYWHERE.' This offers a chance to engage with the artist and his work.

events.title: University Baroque Ensemble & Panel Discussion: John Blow's Venus and Adonis
events.description: The University Baroque Ensemble presents a semi-staged performance of John Blow's Venus and Adonis, preceded by a panel discussion on the opera's mythical themes and historical context.

events.title: Sam Aros-Mitchell: Performance as Ceremony
events.organizer: Department of Theater, Dance, and Performance Studies
events.description: Yaqui choreographer and scholar Sam Aros-Mitchell offers a lecture-performance hybrid, exploring Indigenous futurisms, embodied archives, and the resonances between choreography and Native epistemologies.

events.title: An Evening of Creative Jazz
events.description: An evening dedicated to creative jazz performances by UC Berkeley musicians. This concert showcases innovative and improvisational jazz compositions.

events.title: Berkeley Dance Project 2026 (Opening Night)
events.organizer: Department of Theater, Dance, and Performance Studies
events.description: Opening night of Berkeley Dance Project 2026, celebrating diverse dancemakers with dynamic duets and high-energy group dances choreographed by students, guest artists, and faculty.

events.title: Saint Mary's Gaels at California Golden Bears Baseball
events.location: Evans Diamond at Stu Gordon Stadium, Berkeley, CA
events.description: The Cal Golden Bears baseball team hosts the Saint Mary's Gaels in an exciting home game at Stu Gordon Stadium.

events.title: Brutus Hamilton Multi-Events (Track & Field)
events.description: The California Golden Bears track & field team hosts the Brutus Hamilton Multi-Events at Edwards Stadium.

events.title: Georgia Tech Yellow Jackets at California Golden Bears Baseball
events.location: Evans Diamond at Stu Gordon Stadium, Berkeley, CA
events.description: The Cal Golden Bears baseball team takes on the Georgia Tech Yellow Jackets in a home game at Stu Gordon Stadium.

events.title: Brutus Hamilton Multi-Events (Track & Field)
events.description: Day two of the Brutus Hamilton Multi-Events for the California Golden Bears track & field team, continuing at Edwards Stadium.

events.title: Georgia Tech Yellow Jackets at California Golden Bears Baseball
events.location: Evans Diamond at Stu Gordon Stadium, Berkeley, CA
events.description: The Cal Golden Bears baseball team continues its series against the Georgia Tech Yellow Jackets at Stu Gordon Stadium.

events.title: Cal Football Coaches Clinic
events.description: A clinic for football coaches hosted by Cal Sports Camps, offering insights and training from the university's coaching staff.

events.title: Brutus Hamilton Invitational (Track & Field, Senior Day)
events.description: The final day of the Brutus Hamilton Invitational, featuring Senior Day celebrations for the Cal Track & Field team at Edwards Stadium.

events.title: Cal Men's Gymnastics vs Air Force
events.description: The Cal Men's Gymnastics team competes against Air Force in a home meet at Haas Pavilion.

events.title: Cal Men's Gymnastics vs Oklahoma
events.description: The Cal Men's Gymnastics team hosts Oklahoma for a competitive match at Haas Pavilion.

events.title: Cal Men's Gymnastics vs Stanford
events.description: The Cal Men's Gymnastics team faces off against Stanford in a highly anticipated home meet at Haas Pavilion.

events.title: Post Ramadan Market
events.description: Celebrate the end of Ramadan with a market on Lower Sproul, featuring food, local vendors, and crafts. This event is a partnership with the Muslim Student Association and other ASUC offices.

events.title: Coffee with Grad Div at the Gate
events.description: An informal coffee gathering hosted by the Graduate Division, offering graduate students a chance to connect and socialize.

events.title: The Library TOTES Appreciates You!
events.description: A student appreciation event hosted by the Library, where graduate or professional students can receive a free library tote.

events.title: Tenured Tranquility: Care Packages, Succulents & Rest
events.organizer: UndocuGrads team (part of Graduate Division events)
events.description: A low-key event for graduate students to decompress, connect with nature, and build personalized self-care toolkits including custom sprays and succulents.

events.title: First-Generation Graduate Student Mixer
events.organizer: Haas and across UC Berkeley (Graduate Division event)
events.description: An evening mixer for first-generation graduate and professional students from Haas and across UC Berkeley, offering community-building, career conversations, and resource sharing.

events.title: Graduate Student Appreciation Patio Party
events.organizer: Graduate Social Club and Grad Division
events.description: Bring friends to this patio party for graduate students, featuring tasty bites, games, and music. The event is hosted in partnership with the Graduate Social Club.

events.title: Spring Painting at the School of Optometry
events.description: An opportunity for graduate students to engage in spring painting, hosted at the School of Optometry. This creative event promotes relaxation and artistic expression.

events.title: Games (Board + Matching) & Pizza + Boba Night
events.description: A fun evening featuring board games, matching games, pizza, and boba. This social event offers a relaxed atmosphere for graduate students to unwind.

events.title: The “PhD-fense” Strategy Night (Game Night)
events.description: A game night themed around 'PhD-fense,' providing a playful and social setting for graduate students to relax and interact.

events.title: Don't Miss Grad Slam 2026!
events.description: A campus-wide competition showcasing graduate student research in engaging three-minute talks. Join in-person to support Berkeley semi-finalists and celebrate graduate student research.

events.title: Crafter Dark – DIY Shirt Patches
events.location: 1st Floor of MLK Jr. Building, Game Zone Area
events.description: Take a break and get creative making your own DIY shirt patches. This event is hosted in partnership with Cub Concerts at Berkeley.

events.title: Movie Night at Headlands - Legally Blonde
events.description: Enjoy an evening screening of 'Legally Blonde,' a charming and empowering film. This movie night is free for all UC Berkeley students.

events.title: Commence Your Career: Connecting Seniors & Alums
events.description: An interactive event connecting graduating seniors with Cal alumni for conversations, networking, and career-building opportunities.

events.title: Drag Bingo
events.description: The final Drag Bingo of the fall semester, promising an unforgettable night of laughter, prizes, and treats hosted by Thee Leggy Pee.

events.title: 2026 First Generation End of Year Celebration
events.location: The Inclusive Excellence Hub, 2515 Channing Way, Berkeley, CA 94704
events.description: Join first-generation graduate students, faculty, staff, and allies to celebrate the academic year's end. Dinner and Berkeley First-Generation T-shirts will be provided.

events.title: OSPO Monthly Knowledge Exchange: April
events.description: The monthly Knowledge Exchange hosted by the Open Source Program Office (OSPO) at BIDS. This session facilitates discussions on open science and data practices.

events.title: 2026 Berkeley Corporate + Climate Summit
events.location: Spieker Forum at Berkeley Haas – Chou Hall
events.description: A summit focusing on corporate strategies and legal frameworks related to climate change, bringing together leaders from law and business.

events.title: Dissertation Talk: Interpreting and Controlling Generative Models
events.description: A dissertation talk on the interpretation and control of generative models, presenting new research in AI and machine learning.

events.title: EECS Colloquium | Causal Inference and Decompositions using AI Models: Statistical Theory and Applications to Worker Transitions
events.description: This colloquium explores causal inference and decompositions using AI models, focusing on statistical theory and applications to worker transitions.

events.title: Building Open Connected Scientific Data Products for the Cryosphere Hackdays
events.description: A hackday event focused on developing open and connected scientific data products for cryosphere research. This collaborative workshop supports environmental data science.

events.title: 11th Annual BayViro Symposium
events.organizer: UC Berkeley School of Public Health
events.description: The 11th Annual BayViro Symposium brings together researchers and public health professionals to discuss the latest advancements in virology and related public health issues.

events.title: EECS Colloquium | Physical AI and the Intelligence of Things
events.description: This colloquium explores the concepts of physical AI and the intelligence of things, delving into embedded intelligence and pervasive computing.

events.title: What is Data Art? Colloquium
events.description: A colloquium introducing Berkeley faculty at the heart of Human Technology Futures (HTF), exploring research, questions, and methods in the emerging field of data art.

events.title: Dissertation Talk: Quantum One-Time Programs
events.description: A dissertation talk on quantum one-time programs, exploring advanced concepts in quantum computing and cryptography.

events.title: BETR/EECS Solid State Technologies Seminar
events.description: A seminar on solid state technologies, presented jointly by BETR and EECS. The event features leading research and developments in the field.

events.title: Earth Day Celebration at The Lawrence Hall of Science
events.description: Join community partners and UC Berkeley student organizations for the third-annual Earth Day celebration. Enjoy hands-on science activities, explore climate science, and learn about Ohlone culture.

events.title: BSAC Technology Seminar: Prof. Aditya Varma Muppala
events.description: Professor Aditya Varma Muppala delivers a BSAC Technology Seminar, focusing on advancements in millimeter-wave imaging and metrology.

events.title: CS Area Mentor and Supervisor Orientation (CS Week Q2)
events.description: An orientation for mentors and supervisors within the Computing Sciences Area, held during CS Week Q2. This event supports leadership development in tech.

events.title: CS Area-wide meeting (CS Week Q2)
events.description: A comprehensive area-wide meeting for the Computing Sciences Area, held as part of CS Week Q2. This gathering promotes collaboration and updates within the department.

events.title: Spring 2026 Applied AI Bootcamp for Lawyers
events.description: An intensive bootcamp for legal professionals on applied artificial intelligence, focusing on the practical implications and applications of AI in the legal field.

events.title: College of Computing, Data Science, and Society Undergraduate Commencement Ceremonies (Morning)
events.organizer: College of Computing, Data Science, and Society
events.description: The morning undergraduate commencement ceremony for the College of Computing, Data Science, and Society, celebrating graduates in Computer Science, Data Science, and Statistics.

events.title: College of Computing, Data Science, and Society Undergraduate Commencement Ceremonies (Afternoon)
events.organizer: College of Computing, Data Science, and Society
events.description: The afternoon undergraduate commencement ceremony for the College of Computing, Data Science, and Society, honoring graduates from various computing and data science disciplines.

events.title: UC Berkeley AI Hackathon 4.0
events.description: The fourth iteration of the UC Berkeley AI Hackathon, bringing together students and innovators to develop solutions using artificial intelligence.

events.title: Berkeley Agentic AI Summit
events.organizer: Berkeley RDI (Center for Responsible, Decentralized Intelligence)
events.description: A two-day conference hosted by Berkeley RDI, dedicated exclusively to agentic AI, covering foundation models, frameworks, deployment, and safety.

events.title: CS Area Poster Session (CS Week Q3)
events.description: A poster session for the Computing Sciences Area, showcasing research and projects by faculty, staff, and students. This event is part of CS Week Q3.

events.title: CS Area-wide meeting (CS Week Q3)
events.description: An area-wide meeting for the Computing Sciences Area during CS Week Q3, fostering collaboration and communication among the community.

events.title: CS Area-wide meeting Q4
events.description: The fourth quarterly area-wide meeting for the Computing Sciences Area, providing updates and discussions on departmental activities.

events.title: Berkeley SkyDeck's Demo Day
events.description: Berkeley SkyDeck's Demo Day showcases a new cohort of global founders presenting technologies in AI, healthcare, enterprise software, and climate technology to investors and industry leaders.

events.title: Outside Your Comfort Zone
events.description: Outside Your Comfort Zone

events.title: UC LAUNCH Launchathon
events.description: A two-day hackathon weekend event filled with workshops and networking, where students help UC LAUNCH Startups build their ideas.

events.title: International Founder Roadmap: F-1, CPT/OPT & Beyond
events.description: International Founder Roadmap: F-1, CPT/OPT & Beyond

events.title: Berkeley VC & Startup Summit
events.description: A summit bringing together venture capitalists and startup founders for networking, insights, and discussions on the latest trends in the startup ecosystem.

events.title: Problem of the Month: Framing the Problem
events.description: Problem of the Month: Framing the Problem

events.title: Problem of the Month: Empathy & Ideation
events.description: Problem of the Month: Empathy & Ideation

events.title: Berkeley Haas Alumni Conference (Entrepreneurship Ecosystem track)
events.description: The annual Berkeley Haas Alumni Conference features concurrent sessions on various topics, including a track dedicated to the entrepreneurship ecosystem.

events.title: Global Venture Lab - AI Summit in Entrepreneurship Education
events.organizer: UC Berkeley Sutardja Center for Entrepreneurship & Technology (SCET)
events.description: A two-day summit focusing on AI's impact on entrepreneurship education, bringing together experts to discuss leveraging AI in the classroom and advising students on ethical uses.

events.title: UC LAUNCH Demo Day
events.description: Witness the next wave of entrepreneurs shaping the future at UC LAUNCH Demo Day, where startup finalists pitch for prizes and showcase their ventures.

events.title: Collider Cup XVIII: Berkeley's Premier Student Startup Pitch Competition
events.organizer: UC Berkeley Sutardja Center for Entrepreneurship & Technology (SCET)
events.description: Top student teams pitch their projects to professors, investors, and industry experts for a chance to win the grand prize at SCET's Collider Cup.

events.title: SCET Startup Expo
events.organizer: UC Berkeley Sutardja Center for Entrepreneurship & Technology (SCET)
events.description: Showcase your startup venture at the SCET Startup Expo, where student teams present their innovations to administrators, instructors, and industry guests.
